purpose: Service-type analysis of Alberta ministry maps. Each type is a (service task × Kate Tarling intent) cluster validated by the leverage bar (5+ services across 3+ ministries). Per-gap and per-proposed-example priority signals applied, layered from mechanical leverage and curated value-weighting (reach, financial value, need, dependency).

kate_tarling_intents.name: Getting financial support or claiming something

service_types.name: Appeal a decision
service_types.priority_rationale: Service-type weight: 10 services across 8 ministries (mechanical: low). Value-weighting: medium — Reach: low but high need when invoked. Citizens' safety net when initial decisions go wrong.
service_types.shared_shape: Citizen references a prior decision they want overturned, submits grounds (procedural error, new evidence, wrong on the merits), waits for review by a separate body, receives a new decision (uphold, overturn, or remit). The appellant is heard but doesn't drive the process.
service_types.variations: Administrative appeals (benefits, planning, safety codes) vs. judicial appeals (court decisions). Some go to a separate tribunal, some loop within the same regulator.

gaps.text: No reference-the-original-decision pattern. Appeals start with a specific prior decision and need to link/summarise it.
gaps.priority_rationale: Service-type weight: 10 services across 8 ministries (mechanical: low). Value-weighting: medium — Reach: low but high need when invoked. Citizens' safety net when initial decisions go wrong.

gaps.text: No grounds-for-appeal composer. Appeals need structured reason categories (procedural, factual, new evidence).
gaps.flavor_note: Grounds for appeal: structured event-by-event reasoning, often referencing the original decision's facts.
gaps.priority_rationale: Cross-cutting (describe-what-happened) reach: 3 types (mechanical: medium). Value-weighting: medium — Bounded volume (complaints, appeals, reports). Important for accuracy but not high-reach.

gaps.text: No appeal-status-tracking pattern.
gaps.flavor_note: Distinct appeal milestones; three-branch outcome ahead.
gaps.priority_rationale: Cross-cutting (status-tracking) reach: 8 types (mechanical: high). Value-weighting: high — Reach: every applicant. Spans benefits, licences, grants, appeals, complaints, records requests. High frustration relief on long waits.

gaps.text: No appeal-outcome-with-action pattern (uphold/overturn/remit branches).
gaps.flavor_note: Appeal-outcome variant of results: three-branch decision (uphold/overturn/remit).
gaps.priority_rationale: Cross-cutting (results) reach: 9 types (mechanical: high). Value-weighting: high — Reach: every service interaction ends in a result page of some kind. Five content variants cover decision letters, credentials, records release, appeal outcomes, submission receipts.

proposed_examples.rationale: Loads the original decision, lets the appellant select grounds and write reasoning.
proposed_examples.priority_rationale: Service-type weight: 10 services across 8 ministries (mechanical: low). Value-weighting: medium — Reach: low but high need when invoked. Citizens' safety net when initial decisions go wrong.

proposed_examples.rationale: Status tracker variant for appeals; differs from application status because the next steps and timelines are different.
proposed_examples.flavor_note: Appeal-specific milestones (filed → admitted → heard → decided); three-branch outcome ahead.
proposed_examples.priority_rationale: Cross-cutting (status-tracking) reach: 8 types (mechanical: high). Value-weighting: high — Reach: every applicant. Spans benefits, licences, grants, appeals, complaints, records requests. High frustration relief on long waits.

proposed_examples.rationale: Decision letter pattern with the three possible branches.
proposed_examples.flavor_note: Appeal-outcome variant of results: three-branch (uphold/overturn/remit) with reasoning.
proposed_examples.priority_rationale: Cross-cutting (results) reach: 9 types (mechanical: high). Value-weighting: high — Reach: every service interaction ends in a result page of some kind. Five content variants cover decision letters, credentials, records release, appeal outcomes, submission receipts.

service_types.name: Request government records or action
service_types.priority_rationale: Service-type weight: 16 services across 8 ministries (mechanical: low). Value-weighting: medium — Reach: medium (thousands of FOI requests, lawyer reviews per year). Need: democratic accountability and personal records access.
service_types.shared_shape: Citizen submits a formal request for government records, information, or specific action (inquiry, mediation, restitution, impact statement). Triage and acceptance, processing by the appropriate body, response or release issued. Distinct from search-a-public-register because there's a process; distinct from apply-for-a-benefit because no eligibility/payment shape.
service_types.variations: Information requests (FOI, your own personal records) vs. specific-action requests (fatality inquiry, lawyer fee review, mediation) vs. participation requests (community/victim impact statements). Most are JUS-led; CPE/SA handle FOI; INFRA, TEC have niche request paths.

gaps.text: No request-form pattern with required-info checklist (most have specific data needs).
gaps.priority_rationale: Service-type weight: 16 services across 8 ministries (mechanical: low). Value-weighting: medium — Reach: medium (thousands of FOI requests, lawyer reviews per year). Need: democratic accountability and personal records access.

gaps.text: No status-of-request pattern. Many requests have legislated timelines (FOI is 30 days). Citizens need to track.
gaps.flavor_note: Legislated timeline surfaced (FOI 30 days); status step must include the deadline.
gaps.priority_rationale: Cross-cutting (status-tracking) reach: 8 types (mechanical: high). Value-weighting: high — Reach: every applicant. Spans benefits, licences, grants, appeals, complaints, records requests. High frustration relief on long waits.

gaps.text: No release-of-records pattern. After approval, the citizen receives the records — what does that page look like?
gaps.flavor_note: Records-release variant of results: includes the records themselves with redaction explanation if applicable.
gaps.priority_rationale: Cross-cutting (results) reach: 9 types (mechanical: high). Value-weighting: high — Reach: every service interaction ends in a result page of some kind. Five content variants cover decision letters, credentials, records release, appeal outcomes, submission receipts.

proposed_examples.rationale: Form with required information checklist plus optional fields, fee handling, and consent.
proposed_examples.priority_rationale: Service-type weight: 16 services across 8 ministries (mechanical: low). Value-weighting: medium — Reach: medium (thousands of FOI requests, lawyer reviews per year). Need: democratic accountability and personal records access.

proposed_examples.flavor_note: Legislated-timeline flavor: surfaces the statutory deadline alongside current step.
proposed_examples.priority_rationale: Cross-cutting (status-tracking) reach: 8 types (mechanical: high). Value-weighting: high — Reach: every applicant. Spans benefits, licences, grants, appeals, complaints, records requests. High frustration relief on long waits.

service_types.name: File a complaint
service_types.priority_rationale: Service-type weight: 20 services across 12 ministries (mechanical: medium). Value-weighting: medium — Reach: medium. Need: regulatory accountability matters but not life-or-death.
service_types.shared_shape: Citizen files a formal complaint about a regulated party (professional, business, government decision-maker). Triage and acceptance, investigation by the regulator, decision communicated, possible appeal path. The complainant is heard but doesn't drive the process.
service_types.variations: Regulated-profession complaints (health, security, locksmith) vs. business marketplace complaints (consumer protection) vs. systemic complaints (human rights, police conduct, privacy) vs. specific party (guardian/trustee). Investigation rigour varies.

gaps.text: No 'describe what happened' pattern with timeline support. Complaints often need a sequence of events.
gaps.flavor_note: Complaint timeline: event-by-event with dates, often spanning months.
gaps.priority_rationale: Cross-cutting (describe-what-happened) reach: 3 types (mechanical: medium). Value-weighting: medium — Bounded volume (complaints, appeals, reports). Important for accuracy but not high-reach.

gaps.text: No regulated-party-search pattern. The citizen is filing against a regulated party — they need to find/identify that party.
gaps.flavor_note: Embedded lookup inside the complaint flow: search the doctor / business / officer registry to identify the target of the complaint.
gaps.priority_rationale: Cross-cutting (look-up-and-pick) reach: 4 types (mechanical: high). Value-weighting: high — Sub-pattern but widely embedded. Useful in complaints, renewals, reports, find-place flows.

gaps.text: No status-update notification pattern. Investigations take months.
gaps.flavor_note: Long-running investigation: periodic status updates over months.
gaps.priority_rationale: Cross-cutting (service-messages) reach: 5 types (mechanical: high). Value-weighting: high — Reach: every applicant receives at least one. Decision letters, renewal reminders, clarification requests, status updates.

gaps.text: No outcome-letter pattern (current result-page is too thin for complaint outcomes).
gaps.flavor_note: Decision-letter variant for complaints: long-form reasoning, next steps, appeal info.

service_types.shared_shape: Citizen pushes information about a hazard, suspected harm, or third-party concern. No assessment of the citizen's eligibility — the service is the act of reporting. Acknowledgment is usually sent; investigation may follow with no further citizen involvement.

service_types.name: Apply for a licence or permit
service_types.priority_rationale: Service-type weight: 49 services across 15 ministries (mechanical: medium). Value-weighting: high — Reach: high (driver's licences alone touch millions; plus professional, business, activity permits). Need: high (daily-life-critical credentials).
service_types.shared_shape: Citizen or business demonstrates qualification (training, fitness, fee paid, premises inspected), submits application with evidence, waits for decision, receives credential with expiry and conditions. Renewal is a lighter version of the same shape — verify identity and continued eligibility, pay fee, get updated expiry.
service_types.variations: Personal credentials (driver's licence, firearms PAL, professional licences) vs. business credentials (cannabis, security, locksmith) vs. activity permits (timber, fire, oversize load). **Renewal** is a recurring sub-shape: 'Apply for or renew' services (gaming/liquor/cannabis, driver's licence) and standalone 'Renew' services (firearms PAL) follow the same flow as the original application but pre-filled — fewer questions, focus on what's changed.

gaps.text: No declaration/attestation pattern. Most licences require the citizen to declare facts under penalty.
gaps.priority_rationale: Service-type weight: 49 services across 15 ministries (mechanical: medium). Value-weighting: high — Reach: high (driver's licences alone touch millions; plus professional, business, activity permits). Need: high (daily-life-critical credentials).

cross_cutting_gaps.name: Eligibility checking before applying
cross_cutting_gaps.summary: Citizens need to know if they qualify before starting an application. No canonical pattern. Sometimes 4-5 questions yielding yes/no/maybe with reasons; sometimes an embedded calculator in guidance content.
cross_cutting_gaps.value_weight_rationale: Reach: everyone who considers applying asks this first. High frustration relief.
cross_cutting_gaps.priority_rationale: Cross-cutting reach: 3 types (mechanical: medium). Value-weighting: high — Reach: everyone who considers applying asks this first. High frustration relief.

cross_cutting_gaps.name: Status tracking after submission
cross_cutting_gaps.summary: One structural pattern (where is my thing in its journey, what's expected, what can I act on) with per-type content flavors. result-page covers the immediate confirmation, not the long wait. Must include 'and now what?' affordances when the citizen can act (apply for deferral, dispute, withdraw).
cross_cutting_gaps.value_weight_rationale: Reach: every applicant. Spans benefits, licences, grants, appeals, complaints, records requests. High frustration relief on long waits.

flavors.note: Long-running decision tracking with clarification-request branch.
Multi-phase tracking: submission → review → award → reporting.
Reference number lookup; investigation may not loop back to reporter.
Long-running investigation status surfacing; months not days.
Distinct appeal milestones; three-branch outcome ahead.
This whole service type is the status surface; primarily the 'and now what?' affordance side.

cross_cutting_gaps.priority_rationale: Cross-cutting reach: 8 types (mechanical: high). Value-weighting: high — Reach: every applicant. Spans benefits, licences, grants, appeals, complaints, records requests. High frustration relief on long waits.

cross_cutting_gaps.name: Save and return across sessions
cross_cutting_gaps.summary: Long applications can't be done in one sitting; the public-form template assumes one session. Couples with my-account for the partial-fill state landing.
cross_cutting_gaps.value_weight_rationale: Reach: anyone filling a long form. Means-tested benefit applications run 30-60 minutes; many citizens can't finish in one sitting.
cross_cutting_gaps.priority_rationale: Cross-cutting reach: 3 types (mechanical: medium). Value-weighting: high — Reach: anyone filling a long form. Means-tested benefit applications run 30-60 minutes; many citizens can't finish in one sitting.

cross_cutting_gaps.name: My account home
cross_cutting_gaps.summary: Authenticated home where citizens see their stuff (applications, status, history, records) and act on it (renew, dispute, download). Distinct as a pattern from the `account/portal` productType candidate, though the two converge in commitment.
cross_cutting_gaps.value_weight_rationale: Reach: every authenticated citizen. Anchors the account/portal productType candidate.
cross_cutting_gaps.priority_rationale: Cross-cutting reach: 5 types (mechanical: high). Value-weighting: high — Reach: every authenticated citizen. Anchors the account/portal productType candidate.

cross_cutting_gaps.name: Payment
cross_cutting_gaps.summary: Pay an amount as part of a service interaction. Couples with licence applications and renewals, paid registry searches, fees and fines. Distinct as a pattern from the `payment` productType candidate (which would be the whole pay-a-fee surface).
cross_cutting_gaps.value_weight_rationale: Financial: billions/year. Includes traffic fines, fuel tax, paid registry searches, licence application fees.
cross_cutting_gaps.priority_rationale: Cross-cutting reach: 3 types (mechanical: medium). Value-weighting: high — Financial: billions/year. Includes traffic fines, fuel tax, paid registry searches, licence application fees.

cross_cutting_gaps.name: Messages from government
cross_cutting_gaps.summary: Emails, texts, in-app messages government sends citizens when state changes (we received it, we need more info, your decision is ready, your licence expires in 30 days). No template exists for how these are structured (subject, body, call-to-action, link to landing page).
cross_cutting_gaps.value_weight_rationale: Reach: every applicant receives at least one. Decision letters, renewal reminders, clarification requests, status updates.
cross_cutting_gaps.priority_rationale: Cross-cutting reach: 5 types (mechanical: high). Value-weighting: high — Reach: every applicant receives at least one. Decision letters, renewal reminders, clarification requests, status updates.

cross_cutting_gaps.name: Results
cross_cutting_gaps.summary: One parent pattern (what happened, what's next, contact path) with content variants based on what the result is. The current `result-page` is the only candidate in the design system and is thin for the breadth of cases.
cross_cutting_gaps.value_weight_rationale: Reach: every service interaction ends in a result page of some kind. Five content variants cover decision letters, credentials, records release, appeal outcomes, submission receipts.

variants.name: Submission receipt
variants.summary: We received your thing; here's your reference number. Sent immediately at submission. Light content.

variants.name: Decision letter
variants.summary: Outcome of an application with reasoning and next steps. Long-form.

variants.name: Issued credential
variants.summary: Outcome plus a granted thing (licence, certificate). Print/save/wallet-add affordances.

variants.name: Records release
variants.summary: Outcome IS the records themselves, often with redaction explanation.

variants.name: Appeal outcome
variants.summary: Three-branch decision (uphold / overturn / remit) with reasoning.

cross_cutting_gaps.priority_rationale: Cross-cutting reach: 9 types (mechanical: high). Value-weighting: high — Reach: every service interaction ends in a result page of some kind. Five content variants cover decision letters, credentials, records release, appeal outcomes, submission receipts.

cross_cutting_gaps.name: Submit supporting documents
cross_cutting_gaps.summary: Multi-document checklist with upload status. 'Here are the N documents we need; here's which you've uploaded; here's what's missing.' Distinct from a single file-upload field.
cross_cutting_gaps.value_weight_rationale: Reach: high. Common in grant applications, reports, complaints, records requests, benefit applications, licence applications. High friction point when not handled well.
cross_cutting_gaps.priority_rationale: Cross-cutting reach: 6 types (mechanical: high). Value-weighting: high — Reach: high. Common in grant applications, reports, complaints, records requests, benefit applications, licence applications. High friction point when not handled well.

cross_cutting_gaps.name: Describe what happened, in order
cross_cutting_gaps.summary: Structured event-sequence input where the citizen recounts a sequence of events. Date-tagged segments, event-by-event, ordered. Distinct from a free-text textarea.
cross_cutting_gaps.value_weight_rationale: Bounded volume (complaints, appeals, reports). Important for accuracy but not high-reach.
cross_cutting_gaps.priority_rationale: Cross-cutting reach: 3 types (mechanical: medium). Value-weighting: medium — Bounded volume (complaints, appeals, reports). Important for accuracy but not high-reach.

cross_cutting_gaps.name: Look up and pick (inside a flow)
cross_cutting_gaps.summary: Embedded entity lookup as a step inside another flow: find the doctor you're complaining about, look up your existing licence to renew, identify the regulated party. Distinct from the `directory` productType which is the whole shape.
cross_cutting_gaps.value_weight_rationale: Sub-pattern but widely embedded. Useful in complaints, renewals, reports, find-place flows.
cross_cutting_gaps.priority_rationale: Cross-cutting reach: 4 types (mechanical: high). Value-weighting: high — Sub-pattern but widely embedded. Useful in complaints, renewals, reports, find-place flows.

excluded_or_below_bar.reason: Below leverage bar (4 services, 3 ministries). Worth re-checking when more consultation services are added or another jurisdiction included.
7 services concentrated in EM (energy_minerals); below ministry-spread bar of 3+ ministries with meaningful counts. Alberta-specific industrial pattern, not a general design system service type.
Borderline; only 4-6 candidate services across ~3 ministries (campsite reservation, road test, transportation booking). Below leverage bar. Watch for growth.

source.method: phase-1-classification of 637 supporting services by primary service task (channel-based with name-pattern overrides), then phase-2 clustering by name pattern within each task with leverage bar applied. A critical-pass dedup followed: per-type gaps and proposed_examples that duplicate cross-cutting patterns now carry cross_cutting_refs and flavor_note fields. Priority is computed from two layered signals — mechanical (service-type weight or cross-cutting reach) and value-weighting (curated per type and per cross-cutting using reach, financial flow, need, dependency). When value_weight is set, it overrides the mechanical default and the rationale surfaces both reasons.
constraints_applied: Citizen-side only. Worker-side service types (intake-and-assess, case management) deferred to a follow-up brief.
Product demo patterns NOT used as input to avoid biasing the system view.
Size-agnostic example proposals — captures the concept rather than the size class.
Value-weighting is a first-pass curation (Tom 2026-05-19, with input from Paul). Refinable as data sources for citizen reach and financial flow become available.
ALSS pilot tie-in not applied — pilot service pick happening over the next two weeks; matching service type's priority_rationale gets a targeted amendment once known.
